explanations.5: This applies a blur effect and makes colors more vivid to the background; it's being overridden by line 9, so you can delete it.
explanations.6: This sets a semi-transparent white background, but line 8 overwrites it with a different transparency level, so this line does nothing.
explanations.9: This repeats the blur effect from line 5 with slightly different settings, replacing the earlier one since CSS uses the last rule it encounters.
explanations.10: This overwrites the gray border from line 3 with a more subtle semi-transparent white border that works better with the glassmorphism effect.
explanations.16: The transition property smooths out changes to three different CSS properties (transform, box-shadow, and filter) over 0.4 seconds when you hover.
explanations.20: This makes the image 5% larger and shifts it up 4 pixels when you hover, creating a lifting effect combined with the shadow change.
explanations.22: On hover, this slightly brightens the image and intensifies its colors to make it feel more vibrant and interactive.
